text: Banks must maintain a minimum capital adequacy ratio of 8% under Basel III
Companies are required to file annual reports with the SEC on Form 10-K within 60 days of fiscal year end for large accelerated filers
The Bank Secrecy Act requires financial institutions to file Currency Transaction Reports for transactions exceeding $10,000
GDPR grants data subjects the right to erasure, also known as the right to be forgotten
The Sarbanes-Oxley Act of 2002 was enacted in response to major corporate accounting scandals including Enron and WorldCom
Under MiFID II, investment firms must provide best execution for client orders
The Volcker Rule prohibits banks from proprietary trading with their own accounts
Basel III introduced a Liquidity Coverage Ratio requiring banks to hold sufficient high-quality liquid assets to cover 30 days of net cash outflows
The Foreign Account Tax Compliance Act (FATCA) requires foreign financial institutions to report accounts held by US taxpayers to the IRS
Under the USA PATRIOT Act, financial institutions must establish Anti-Money Laundering programs
The SEC requires public companies to disclose material information through Form 8-K within four business days of the triggering event
SOX Section 302 requires the CEO and CFO to personally certify the accuracy of financial statements
GDPR requires that personal data breaches be reported to the supervisory authority within 72 hours of becoming aware of the breach
The Dodd-Frank Act created the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB)
Under Basel III, the Common Equity Tier 1 capital ratio minimum is 4.5%
The SEC's Regulation Fair Disclosure (Reg FD) prohibits selective disclosure of material nonpublic information
Know Your Customer (KYC) requirements mandate that financial institutions verify the identity of their clients
The Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) applies to all entities that store, process, or transmit cardholder data
Under the Investment Company Act of 1940, mutual funds must register with the SEC
The Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act requires financial institutions to explain their information-sharing practices to customers
SOX Section 404 requires management to assess and report on the effectiveness of internal controls over financial reporting
The Basel Committee on Banking Supervision is headquartered in Basel, Switzerland
GDPR applies to any organization processing personal data of EU residents, regardless of where the organization is based
The SEC's Rule 10b-5 prohibits fraud in connection with the purchase or sale of securities
Under AML regulations, Suspicious Activity Reports (SARs) must be filed within 30 days of initial detection of suspicious activity
The Dodd-Frank Act requires systemically important financial institutions to submit living wills to regulators
The Fair Credit Reporting Act gives consumers the right to access their credit reports
Under MiFID II, investment firms must record all telephone conversations and electronic communications relating to client orders
The Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) is the primary regulator of national banks in the United States
GDPR imposes fines of up to 4% of annual global turnover or 20 million euros, whichever is greater, for the most serious violations
The Financial Action Task Force (FATF) sets international standards for combating money laundering and terrorist financing
Under the Securities Act of 1933, new securities offerings must be registered with the SEC unless an exemption applies
The Basel III Net Stable Funding Ratio requires banks to maintain a stable funding profile in relation to their assets and off-balance-sheet activities
The Community Reinvestment Act encourages banks to meet the credit needs of all segments of their communities, including low- and moderate-income neighborhoods
Under Reg NMS, the Order Protection Rule requires trading centers to execute orders at the best available price
The Bank for International Settlements was established in 1930
GDPR requires organizations to appoint a Data Protection Officer when they carry out large-scale systematic monitoring of individuals
The Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) regulates the US derivatives markets, including futures and swaps
Under SOX, audit committees of public companies must be composed entirely of independent directors
The Dodd-Frank Act requires that standardized OTC derivatives be cleared through central counterparties
The Dodd-Frank Act was enacted in 2015 to address the 2008 financial crisis
Insider trading is legal in the United States if disclosed to the SEC within 48 hours
Banks are not required to maintain any minimum capital reserves under international regulations
The SEC was established in 1975 as part of the Securities Reform Act
GDPR only applies to companies physically headquartered within the European Union
Money laundering is only a criminal offense if the amount exceeds $1 million
Public companies in the US are only required to file financial reports once every two years
The Volcker Rule permits banks to engage in unlimited proprietary trading
Cryptocurrency exchanges are completely exempt from all AML and KYC regulations globally
The Sarbanes-Oxley Act only applies to private companies, not publicly traded ones
Under US law, financial advisors have no fiduciary duty to their clients
The Federal Reserve was established in 1945 after World War II
FATCA allows US taxpayers to hold unlimited unreported assets in foreign accounts
The Basel Accords are voluntary guidelines with no enforcement mechanism whatsoever
Companies can freely engage in price-fixing as long as all parties agree voluntarily
Tax evasion and tax avoidance are legally identical under US federal law
The FDIC insures bank deposits up to $5 million per depositor per institution
Short selling of securities is illegal in all developed financial markets
There are no penalties for filing false financial statements with the SEC
Hedge funds are required to be registered as public companies with the SEC
The Glass-Steagall Act was never repealed and remains fully in force today
Credit rating agencies are government organizations run by the US Treasury
The European Central Bank has no role in supervising eurozone banks
Ponzi schemes are legal as long as initial investors receive their promised returns
Financial institutions are permitted to discriminate based on race when making lending decisions
Central banks have no authority to set interest rates or monetary policy
The World Bank was created to regulate stock exchanges across all nations
Corporate officers face no personal liability for securities fraud under any circumstances
The Bank Secrecy Act was repealed in 2020 and no longer applies
Whistleblower protections under Dodd-Frank only apply to government employees, not private sector workers
Basel III requires a minimum leverage ratio of 5% for all internationally active banks
SOX Section 404 compliance requirements only apply to companies with over $1 billion in annual revenue
Under GDPR, organizations must appoint a Data Protection Officer regardless of the nature or scale of their data processing activities
The Dodd-Frank Act requires all banks to conduct annual stress tests, regardless of asset size
The SEC requires insider trading reports on Form 4 to be filed within 5 business days of the transaction
Basel III's countercyclical capital buffer can be set between 0% and 5% of risk-weighted assets
Under MiFID II, the maximum allowable dark pool trading volume for a single venue is 8% of total EU volume in that instrument
The Volcker Rule permits banks to invest up to 5% of Tier 1 capital in hedge funds and private equity
FATCA reporting thresholds for foreign financial assets begin at $25,000 for individual taxpayers residing in the United States
The Bank Secrecy Act requires financial institutions to maintain transaction records for a minimum of 3 years
Under Reg FD, companies have a 48-hour window to publicly disclose unintentional selective disclosures of material information
SOX requires external auditors to rotate the lead audit partner every 3 years
The FDIC insurance limit was raised to $300,000 per depositor as part of the Dodd-Frank Act
Under the Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act, customers must actively opt in before a financial institution can share their information with non-affiliated third parties
The SEC requires quarterly reports on Form 10-Q to be filed within 30 days of the quarter's end for all public companies
Basel III requires a minimum Total Capital ratio of 10% of risk-weighted assets
GDPR defines personal data breach notification to affected individuals as mandatory within 48 hours of discovery
The Dodd-Frank whistleblower program guarantees a minimum reward of 15% of sanctions collected over $1 million
MiFID II's transaction reporting obligation requires reports to be submitted within 48 hours of the transaction
Under the USA PATRIOT Act, enhanced due diligence is required for all accounts exceeding $50,000 in value
The Community Reinvestment Act requires banks to allocate at least 10% of their lending to low-income communities
SEC Rule 144 requires a 12-month holding period before restricted securities can be resold
Under SOX, the maximum criminal penalty for securities fraud is 15 years imprisonment
The Dodd-Frank Act reduced the SEC's enforcement authority over credit rating agencies
Basel III's Liquidity Coverage Ratio requires banks to hold enough liquid assets to survive 60 days of financial stress
GDPR requires all data transfers outside the EU to be pre-approved by the European Data Protection Board
The Foreign Corrupt Practices Act only applies to payments made to officials of OECD member countries
Under Regulation S-P, financial institutions must provide privacy notices to customers annually and whenever their privacy policy changes
The Dodd-Frank Act mandates that all swap dealers maintain a minimum net capital of $50 million
Basel III allows Additional Tier 1 capital instruments to count for up to 2.5% of risk-weighted assets
